title: Record bootstrap interactive wizard decision brief
description: Capture the grill-with-docs decisions for adding an interactive bootstrap wizard, global skill preflight, and existing-project retrofit semantics.

title: Refresh recommended global skills
description: Ensure reusable Codex Bootstrap Protocol workflow skills are present under /home/echo/.agents/skills and update existing global copies from this repo.
close_reason: Updated recommended global skills from repo source via one subagent per skill: brainstormer, grill-with-docs, product-architect, project-planner, plan-to-beads-unified, tdd, and minion. Verified global files match source byte-for-byte.

title: Document new project bootstrap workflow
description: Add explicit README steps for bootstrapping a new target project and the expected Codex Bootstrap Protocol workflow after install.
close_reason: README now documents the end-to-end new-project bootstrap sequence, verification checks, and expected target workflow from orientation through Git sync.

title: Disable Dolt sync workflow in project instructions
description: The user only wants normal Git sync for this repository. Update Beads/Codex instructions and project config so agents do not require or attempt bd dolt push, while preserving the supported bd storage backend and JSONL export.
close_reason: Disabled Dolt remote-sync workflow for this repo by setting no-push=true and replacing bd dolt push/pull instructions with normal git push guidance. bd 1.0.4 still uses embedded Dolt internally, so this disables Dolt sync requirements rather than changing the local storage backend.

title: Resolve project-plan review blockers
description: Remove Antigravity implementation commits, re-own the Beads backlog, reopen affected work as needed, and update the project-plan review status.
close_reason: Antigravity implementation commits were removed from branch history; T001/T002 were reopened; operator confirmed GitHub username FancyP4nda and email zjones976@gmail.com as the intended Beads owner identity; project-plan artifacts now record approved status.

title: Cross-model review project plan atomic Beads
description: Run cross-model review on docs/codex-bootstrap-protocol-project-plan.md with focus on atomic fanout-ready Beads, log findings, and draft/apply proposed documentation changes.
close_reason: Cross-model project-plan review completed. Technical atomicity fixes were applied to docs and Beads; final approval remains blocked on operator decisions recorded in docs/codex-bootstrap-protocol-project-plan-review-log.md.

title: T012: Run final acceptance validation and fix gaps
description:
Source plan: docs/codex-bootstrap-protocol-project-plan.md
Parent PRD: docs/codex-bootstrap-protocol-PRD.md
Plan task: T012
Epic: E04
Triage: backlog (bridge-created; awaiting scribe-refine)

Collision domain: Whole repo, final verification, `/tmp` disposable target.
Parallel-safe: No
Can run with: None
Must not run with: All implementation tasks

## Template
Chore

## Summary
Execute validation, fix discovered gaps, and leave a concise verification record.

## Changes Needed
- What to build: Execute validation, fix discovered gaps, and leave a concise verification record.
- Context to read: Full PRD; this project plan; verification docs/scripts; `./bootstrap --help`.
- Expected public interface: Final acceptance evidence for the scaffold repo.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Shell syntax checks pass.
- [ ] Reference scans show no live `.claude/*` runtime dependencies in installed Codex runtime assets, with documented allowlists for migration/source references.
- [ ] Relocation scan reports no hardcoded `/home/echo/ACC` runtime dependency outside allowed examples, migration notes, `.archive/`, and review logs.
- [ ] Dry-run output matches expected file plan.
- [ ] Missing-`bd` behavior fails before writes.
- [ ] Disposable `/tmp` install/readback confirms the expected tree when `bd` is available.
- [ ] Any skipped validation is explained with the exact command to run after prerequisites are met.

## API Contract / Frontend Component
Final acceptance evidence for the scaffold repo.

## Scope Boundaries
- Constraints: Do not delete unrelated `/tmp` or workspace content; only clean clearly test-created directories when safe; do not perform external actions.
- Dependencies: T006, T007, T008, T009, T010, T011.
- Parallelization: Parallel-safe: No. Collision domain: entire repo and final acceptance state. Can run with None. Must not run with all implementation tasks.
- Collision domain: Whole repo, final verification, `/tmp` disposable target.
- Can run with: None
- Must not run with: All implementation tasks

## Testing Strategy
The finalized verification command set from T010, plus `./bootstrap --dry-run /tmp/codex-bootstrap-smoke --prefix CBS` and a real `/tmp` install when `bd` is available.

## PRD Traceability
Traceability: docs/codex-bootstrap-protocol-PRD.md All Must-priority FRs, AC-001 through AC-021, M-001 through M-003.

## Closeout Criteria
Acceptance evidence is recorded, blockers are explicit, and the plan is ready for `plan-to-beads-unified`.

## Effort Forecast
- Plan: ~2 turns, ~800 tokens (skim affected files)
- Implement: ~6 turns, ~2500 tokens (per plan's Changes Needed)
- Test: ~2 turns, ~600 tokens (grep / type-check verification)
- Total: ~10 turns, ~3900 tokens
- Confidence: low (auto-derived from task size; not yet calibrated against prior items)
close_reason: T012 acceptance passed; recorded evidence in verification/README.md. Ran static shell/reference/relocation suite, ./bootstrap --help, dry-run/readback, missing-bd fail-before-write simulation, disposable /tmp install/readback with bd, managed-path conflict check, and protected-path check. No validation skipped.

close_reason: T011 complete: bootstrap now installs the final manifest-driven asset tree including workflow skills, artifact templates, custom agents, opt-in docs, and Beads; dry-run reports the same managed tree and install summary points to AGENTS.md, docs/opt-in-configs.md, Beads status, and next workflow skills. title: T009: Add Codex custom agents for delegated roles
description:
Source plan: docs/codex-bootstrap-protocol-project-plan.md
Parent PRD: docs/codex-bootstrap-protocol-PRD.md
Plan task: T009
Epic: E03
Triage: backlog (review-corrected; awaiting scribe-refine)
Classification: size/cynefin/persona/layer = TBD (scribe-refine)
Collision domain: `.codex/agents/*.toml`, agent role docs, references from session/minion skills.
Parallel-safe: Yes
Can run with: T007, T010
Must not run with: T008

## Template
Small Feature

## Summary
Narrow TOML custom-agent role definitions. This task owns `.codex/agents/worker.toml`; the worker role is constrained to report-only `minion` v1 behavior.

## Changes Needed
- What to build: Add project-scoped custom agent TOML files for repeatable delegated roles such as `navigator`, `reviewer`, report-only `worker`, and optional `scribe`.
- Context to read: PRD AI-001, FR-008, Q-003; Decision Brief custom-agent split; official Codex custom-agent documentation cited in PRD section 14; source navigator/scribe/quartermaster/herald agents under `../bootstrap-protocol/.claude/agents/` as conceptual inputs only.
- Expected public interface: Codex custom agents available to spawned subagent workflows.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `.codex/agents/navigator.toml` exists with `name`, `description`, and `developer_instructions`.
- [ ] `.codex/agents/reviewer.toml` exists with correctness/security/test-gap review instructions.
- [ ] `.codex/agents/worker.toml` or equivalent exists for `minion` report-only review/research workers; write-capable implementation workers are out of scope for v1.
- [ ] Optional `scribe` role is included only if session-wrapup delegates doc maintenance.
- [ ] Agent files avoid model/provider/auth assumptions unless explicitly needed and documented.

## API Contract / Frontend Component
Codex custom agents available to spawned subagent workflows.

## Scope Boundaries
- Constraints: Include required TOML fields; avoid setting model/provider/auth defaults; keep roles narrow.
- Dependencies: T004.
- Parallelization: Parallel-safe: Yes. Collision domain: `.codex/agents`. Can run with T007, T010. Must not run with T008 because T009 owns `worker.toml` consumed by `minion`.
- Collision domain: `.codex/agents/*.toml`.
- Can run with: T007, T010
- Must not run with: T008

## Testing Strategy
`rg -n "name =|description =|developer_instructions|model_provider|openai_base_url" .codex/agents`; `python3 -c "import pathlib, tomllib; [tomllib.load(open(p, 'rb')) for p in pathlib.Path('.codex/agents').glob('*.toml')]"`.

## PRD Traceability
Traceability: docs/codex-bootstrap-protocol-PRD.md FR-006, FR-008, AI-001, Q-003.

## Closeout Criteria
Required TOML files parse, each has `name`, `description`, and `developer_instructions`, prohibited provider/auth keys are absent, and relevant skills reference the agent names they consume.
close_reason: Added navigator, reviewer, and report-only worker custom-agent TOML files; verified required fields parse and no provider/auth defaults are present.

title: T008: Design and implement `minion` report-only fanout
description:
Source plan: docs/codex-bootstrap-protocol-project-plan.md
Parent PRD: docs/codex-bootstrap-protocol-PRD.md
Plan task: T008
Epic: E03
Triage: backlog (review-corrected; awaiting scribe-refine)
Classification: size/cynefin/persona/layer = TBD (scribe-refine)
Collision domain: `.agents/skills/minion`, `.codex/state/tmp/minion-*`, minion report schema docs.
Parallel-safe: Yes
Can run with: T006, T007, T010
Must not run with: T009

## Template
Medium Feature

## Summary
Report-only Codex subagent fanout workflow and report/state contract. T008 consumes the report-only worker custom agent owned by T009 and does not create custom-agent TOML.

## Changes Needed
- What to build: Create the Codex-native `minion` skill for explicit report-only subagent fanout with default 10-way fanout, no scaffold hard cap, warnings for large fanouts, clear deferral of write-heavy execution to a later write-capable phase, and references to the report-only worker agent owned by T009.
- Context to read: PRD FR-008, R-004, AI-001; T009 worker-agent contract; source Falcon files under `../bootstrap-protocol/.claude/skills/falcon/` only as conceptual input; official Codex subagent documentation cited in PRD section 14.
- Expected public interface: `$minion` Codex skill invocation and structured report summaries.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `minion` skill exists under `.agents/skills/minion`.
- [ ] `minion` defaults to 10-way fanout when no count is specified.
- [ ] `minion` imposes no scaffold-level hard cap and warns that practical limits are subject to Codex runtime/account/environment constraints.
- [ ] Write-heavy fanout is not executed in v1; requests are converted to report-only planning/review or deferred to a later write-capable phase with file-scope and collision-domain planning.
- [ ] Report-only v1 explicitly defers locks, cron/watch, auto-amend, auto-ack, cross-branch orchestration, and PR actions.
- [ ] Transient report path convention is documented under `.codex/state/tmp/minion-*`.
- [ ] `minion` references a report-only worker custom agent but does not create or edit `.codex/agents/worker.toml`.

## API Contract / Frontend Component
`$minion` Codex skill invocation and structured report summaries.

## Scope Boundaries
- Constraints: Do not invoke Claude commands; do not implement background cron/watch; do not create PRs; keep transient state gitignored; do not create custom-agent TOML files.
- Dependencies: T004, T009.
- Parallelization: Parallel-safe: Yes. Collision domain: minion skill and report schema. Can run with T006, T007, T010. Must not run with T009 because T009 owns `.codex/agents/worker.toml`.
- Collision domain: `.agents/skills/minion`, `.codex/state/tmp/minion-*`, minion report schema docs.
- Can run with: T006, T007, T010
- Must not run with: T009

## Testing Strategy
`rg -n "claude --bg|claude agents|claude rm|\\.claude/tmp|auto-amend|auto-ack|cron|PR|write-capable|implementation worker" .agents/skills/minion .codex/agents docs`; expected matches must be explicit deferred-scope statements, not active v1 behavior.

## PRD Traceability
Traceability: docs/codex-bootstrap-protocol-PRD.md FR-008, R-004, AI-001, US-007, AC-015, AC-016, AC-017.

## Closeout Criteria
`minion/SKILL.md` and the report schema doc exist, prohibited active behaviors appear only as deferred-scope statements, and a sample report-only prompt/readback fixture is included.
close_reason: T008 complete: added .agents/skills/minion/SKILL.md, report-schema.md, and a report-only prompt/readback fixture. The skill defaults to 10-way fanout, imposes no scaffold hard cap, warns about Codex runtime/account/environment constraints, references .codex/agents/worker.toml without editing it, documents .codex/state/tmp/minion-* reports, converts write-heavy requests to report-only planning/review or defers write-capable dispatch, and explicitly defers locks, cron/watch, auto-amend, auto-ack, cross-branch orchestration, PR actions, and write-capable implementation workers. Verified required rg scan; minion matches are deferred/report-only statements only.

## Template

## Summary

## Changes Needed
- What to build: Document scaffold purpose, `./bootstrap` usage, optional symlink/PATH setup, Beads prerequisite, install safety behavior, opt-in configs, and relocation expectations.
- Context to read: PRD FR-009, NFR-005, R-003, R-004, R-005, R-006; Decision Brief resolved decisions.
- Expected public interface: Human-readable Markdown docs.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `README.md` explains Codex-native-only scope, primary `./bootstrap` usage, dry-run, force behavior, and Beads prerequisite.
- [ ] `docs/opt-in-configs.md` covers hooks, rules, custom subagents, `minion` settings, stricter approval/sandbox profiles, and any memory or automation examples shipped.
- [ ] Each opt-in config entry includes purpose, risk level, enable steps, disable steps, and verification.

title: T004: Build the Codex-native install asset tree
description:
Source plan: docs/codex-bootstrap-protocol-project-plan.md

Epic: E02
Triage: backlog (review-corrected; awaiting scribe-refine)

Parallel-safe: Yes
Can run with: T002, T005, T006, T010
Must not run with: T003

## Template
Medium Feature

## Summary
Asset tree under `assets/scaffold/` used by the installer for Codex-native target projects.

## Changes Needed
- What to build: Create the files and directories under `assets/scaffold/` that `bootstrap` installs into target repos: Codex instructions, docs stubs, templates, minimal config, opt-in hook/rule examples, state directories, ignore rules, and the managed-path manifest consumed by T003/T011.
- Context to read: PRD FR-004, FR-009, C-005, C-006; Decision Brief target tree; official Codex surface evidence in PRD section 14.
- Expected public interface: Installed target file tree and docs/config surfaces.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scaffold assets include `AGENTS.md`, `docs/CONTEXT.md`, `docs/adr/`, `docs/prd.md`, `docs/project-plan.md`, `docs/architecture.md`, `docs/backend.md`, `docs/frontend.md`, `docs/data-model.md`, `docs/security.md`, `docs/handoff.yaml`, `docs/changelog.yaml`, `docs/enhancements.md`, `docs/standards-history.md`, `.agents/templates/`, `.codex/config.toml`, `.codex/hooks/`, `.codex/rules/`, `.codex/state/tmp/`, `.archive/`.
- [ ] `.codex/config.toml` stays minimal and does not set model, provider, auth, telemetry, network, sandbox, or auto-approval defaults.
- [ ] Transient `.codex/state/tmp/` is gitignored.
- [ ] No install asset creates active `.claude/` runtime directories.
- [ ] `assets/scaffold/manifest.txt` or equivalent records the canonical managed-path list consumed by T003 and T011.

## API Contract / Frontend Component
Installed target file tree and docs/config surfaces.

## Scope Boundaries
- Constraints: Hooks/rules are examples only by default; durable state under `docs/`; transient state under `.codex/state/tmp/`.

- Parallelization: Parallel-safe: Yes. Collision domain: asset tree. Can run with T002, T005, T006, T010. Must not run with T003 due install path integration.
- Collision domain: `assets/scaffold/`.
- Can run with: T002, T005, T006, T010
- Must not run with: T003

## Testing Strategy
`find assets/scaffold -maxdepth 4 -type f -print`; `rg -n "model_provider|openai_base_url|sandbox_mode|approval_policy|\\.claude" assets/scaffold`.

## Closeout Criteria
`assets/scaffold/` exists, the managed-path manifest exists, the verification commands above run, and installer integration notes identify any manifest fields T003/T011 must consume.

title: T003: Add prerequisite, dry-run, conflict, and Beads install flow
description:
Source plan: docs/codex-bootstrap-protocol-project-plan.md
Parent PRD: docs/codex-bootstrap-protocol-PRD.md
Plan task: T003
Epic: E01
Triage: backlog (review-corrected; awaiting scribe-refine)
Classification: size/cynefin/persona/layer = TBD (scribe-refine)
Collision domain: `bootstrap`, install planning logic, conflict detection, Beads initialization.
Parallel-safe: No
Can run with: None
Must not run with: T002, T004

## Template
Medium Feature

## Summary
Installer planning and safety gates around writes, conflicts, Beads, force behavior, and the canonical managed paths under `assets/scaffold/`.

## Changes Needed
- What to build: Complete installer control flow against the `assets/scaffold/` managed-path contract: dry-run planning, mandatory `bd` checks, fail-before-write behavior, conflict reporting, `--force` semantics, and Beads initialization/validation.
- Context to read: PRD FR-002, FR-003, FR-005; T004 output under `assets/scaffold/`; `../bootstrap-protocol/init-project.sh` validation and `bd init` sections.
- Expected public interface: CLI install behavior and operator-facing diagnostics.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Dry-run lists created paths, skipped paths, and conflicts without writes.
- [ ] Missing `bd` causes non-zero exit before target writes for real installs.
- [ ] Existing managed-path conflicts stop before writes by default.
- [ ] Conflict report includes path, planned action, reason, and suggested resolution.
- [ ] `--force` may overwrite managed scaffold files only and never overwrites `.beads/`, `.git/`, credentials, or ignored transient state.
- [ ] Existing `.beads/` is validated rather than replaced.

## API Contract / Frontend Component
CLI install behavior and operator-facing diagnostics.

## Scope Boundaries
- Constraints: Beads mandatory; dry-run does not require Beads; fail before partial writes; no destructive operations against protected paths.
- Dependencies: T002, T004.
- Parallelization: Parallel-safe: No. Collision domain: core CLI control flow. Can run with None. Must not run with T002 and T004.
- Collision domain: `bootstrap`, install planning logic, conflict detection, Beads initialization.
- Can run with: None
- Must not run with: T002, T004

## Testing Strategy
`bash -n ./bootstrap`; `./bootstrap --dry-run /tmp/codex-bootstrap-smoke --prefix CBS`; run a missing-`bd` simulation if practical by invoking with a constrained `PATH`.

## PRD Traceability
Traceability: docs/codex-bootstrap-protocol-PRD.md FR-002, FR-003, FR-005, M-003, US-002, US-003, US-005, AC-004 through AC-011.

## Closeout Criteria
Safety behavior is verified and documented with example outputs or commands.
close_reason: T003 complete: bootstrap now parses assets/scaffold/manifest.txt, reports dry-run created/skipped/conflict paths without bd or writes, requires bd before real target writes, blocks managed-path conflicts before copying, reports path/action/reason/resolution, limits --force to managed regular files while blocking protected .beads/symlink state, initializes missing Beads, and validates existing .beads with bd ping. Verified bash -n, dry-run smoke, missing-bd PATH simulation, conflict no-partial-write, force install, existing .beads validation, and protected .beads symlink blocking.

close_reason: T002 complete: bootstrap parses --help, --prefix, --dry-run, --force; dry-run works without bd and reports bd_required: no / writes: none; source_dir resolves from executable path; bash -n ./bootstrap, ./bootstrap --help, and ./bootstrap --dry-run /tmp/codex-bootstrap-smoke --prefix CBS pass.
